title: A system and method for vertical arrangement of vehicles
abstract: The present invention discloses a means for effective utilization of space during storing and transporting vehicles, and specifically, tractors. The invention comprises a tractor (104) mounted/placed vertically on a supporting frame (102) and a retention unit (106) present on the supporting frame (102) to attach the tractor (104) to the supporting frame (102) and prevent the tractor (104) from slipping downwards. The supporting frame (102) is placed on a base surface (108), wherein the base surface (108) is a storage facility base surface or a transportation truck bed base surface. The supporting frame (102) further comprises an indentation (110) to avoid damage to plastic components protruding from the tractor (104). Multiple tractors (104) are further placed opposite to each other on either side of the supporting frame (102) for effective utilization of space for transportation and/or storage.
claims: Claims ( 16 ) A system for vertical arrangement of vehicles, wherein the system comprises: a base surface (108); at least one supporting frame (102) mounted/placed on the base surface (108) characterized in that the supporting frame (102) is configured to permit at least one vehicle (104) to be positioned vertically on either side of the supporting frame (102); at least one retention unit (106) configured to retain the vehicle (104) on the supporting frame (102). The system for vertical arrangement of vehicles as claimed in claim 1, further comprising rear wheels of the vehicle (104) closer to the base surface (108) and front-wheel(s) of the vehicle (104) are away from the base surface (108) when the vehicle (104) is in a vertical position. The system for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 1, wherein a first vehicle (104/1) is placed vertically on a first side of the supporting frame (102) and a second vehicle (104/2) is placed vertically on a second side of the supporting frame (102). The system for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 3, wherein the second vehicle (104/2) is placed vertically on the second side of the supporting frame (102) such that a part of the second vehicle overlaps a part of the first vehicle (104/1). The system for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 3, wherein the first vehicle (104/1) and the second vehicle (104/2) are placed opposite each other across the supporting frame (102) such that a set of front wheels and a set of rear wheels of the first vehicle (104/1) are aligned with a set of front wheels and a set of rear wheels of the second vehicle (104/2) respectively. The system for vertical arrangement of vehicles as claimed in claim 1, wherein the retention unit (106) is one or more of a metal clutch configured to be wrapped around a set of front wheels of the vehicle (104), a strap made of a sturdy, stretchable material to be wrapped around the vehicle (104) wheels, and magnets to retain a set of front wheels and/or rear wheels on the supporting frame (102). The system for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 2, wherein the rear wheels or the front wheels of the vehicle (104) are touching the base surface (108) such that the entire weight of the vehicle (104) is on the rear wheels or the front wheels when the vehicle (104) is in vertical position. The system for vertical arrangement of vehicles as claimed in claim 1, wherein an indentation (110) is present to accommodate components protruding from the vehicle (104) to prevent damage to the components. A method for a vertical arrangement of vehicles, wherein the method comprises: mounting/placing a supporting frame (102) on a base surface (108) such that the supporting frame (102) is configured to permit at least one vehicle (104) to be positioned vertically on either side of the supporting frame (102); and retaining the vehicle (104) on the supporting frame (102) using a retention unit (130). The method for vertical arrangement of vehicles as claimed in claim 9, further comprising, positioning the vehicle (104) against the supporting frame (102) in a manner that rear wheels of the vehicle (104) closer to the base surface (108) and front-wheel(s) of the vehicle (104) are away from the base surface (108) when vehicle (104) is in vertical position. The method for a vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 10, wherein positioning the vehicle (104) on the base surface (108) against the supporting frame (102) such that the rear wheels or the front wheels of the vehicle (104) touch the base surface (108). The method for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 9, further comprising, placing a first vehicle (104/1) vertically on a first side of the supporting frame (102) and placing a second vehicle (104/2) vertically on a second side of the supporting frame (102). The method for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 12, wherein the method comprises placing the second vehicle (104/2) such that, a part of the second vehicle (104/2) overlaps a part of the first vehicle (104/1). The method for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 12, wherein the method comprises placing the second vehicle (104/2) such that a set of front wheels and a set of rear wheels of the first vehicle (104/1) are aligned with a set of front wheels and a set of rear wheels of the second vehicle (104/2). The method for vertical arrangement of vehicles (104) as claimed in claim 9, wherein the retention unit (106) is one or more of a metal clutch configured to be wrapped around a set of front wheels of the vehicle (104), a strap made of a sturdy, flexible material to be wrapped around the vehicle (104), and magnets to retain a set of front wheels and/or rear wheels on the supporting frame (102). The method for vertical arrangement of vehicles as claimed in claim 9, further comprising placing the vehicle (104) above an indentation (110), wherein the indentation (110) is present to accommodate components protruding from the vehicle (104), and wherein the indentation (110) prevents damage to the components.
